article_meta.title: close Video American Accountability Foundation president releases Education Department 'watch list,' explains why Trump

reasoning: The summary largely attributes charged language to the Department of Education and avoids adopting the source’s partisan framing as fact. It omits the article’s most overtly loaded terms and presents the DOE’s characterizations with attribution. Minor echoing of agency phrasing occurs, but not in the model’s own voice.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral reporting verbs and attributes agency claims; minor reuse of DOE phrasing but with attribution.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: All evaluative statements (meritless, dubious legal theory) are attributed to DOE.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: No subjective judgments stated as fact by the model.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Tone is measured; avoids emotive or alarmist language.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No speculation about motives or intentions.
custom_reasoning.slant: Focus tracks the article’s DOE-centric content; little opposing perspective exists in the source.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Does not cover the AAF ‘watch list’ angle mentioned in the header; summary centers on DOE actions only.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Adjectives used are factual or quoted/attributed.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Politically charged terms appear only in quotes or as DOE’s position; otherwise neutral.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Balance mirrors the source without undue emphasis on negative framing.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: No preferential treatment beyond reflecting DOE’s statements as the primary source.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: More neutral than source; avoids terms like ‘hoax’ and ‘so-called’ in its own voice.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Lists DOE perspective but includes AAF in sources without reflecting it in the summary; outlet (Fox News Digital) isn’t a perspective.
